description: The practice of creating an environment where all individuals are welcomed, respected, supported, and valued to fully participate. It is the active process of making the whole accessible to all its parts.
rationale: The gathering 'we' draws all voices into the circle - not just the loud or the familiar, but every thread needed for the tapestry's completion. This embrace expands through 'tho', the systemic wholeness that recognizes a body needs all its parts to thrive. Together they teach inclusion's wisdom: we are only whole when we are all here, the circle incomplete until the last voice joins the song.
